segments.title: Welcome to Scout
segments.summary: Introduces Scout as a persistent memory and context layer and frames the welcome workspace as a tutorial you can use, replace, or remove.

segments.title: What you're looking at
segments.summary: Explains the substrate shape (index/ and sources/) and introduces line-level provenance as the core property that lets you click any segment and open the exact lines in the original document.

segments.title: Bringing in your own data
segments.summary: The full ingest pipeline: the 5-step Telegram-to-Scout quickstart (with the macOS Telegram Lite requirement called out inline), plus one-liner commands for ChatGPT, Claude (claude.ai), Claude Code, OpenAI Codex CLI, and PDFs. Cowork/Team export is noted as pending.

segments.title: When you are ready to remove this welcome
segments.summary: Three-step removal: point DOSSIER_ROOT at your real substrate, restart the dev server, and the welcome content falls away.
